item.title: Beyond Inflammation - A Multidisciplinary Approach to Managing Obesity and Cardiometabolic Risk in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Current diabetes reports - Review - Adults with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) demonstrate a high prevalence of obesity, metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD) and cardiometabolic comorbidities. Cardiovasc...

item.title: Ultra-processed food intake and risk of inflammatory bowel disease in the adult population ‒ A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Revista espanola de enfermedades digestivas - The incidence of inflammatory bowel disease has risen in recent years. Although its pathophysiology remains unclear, evidence suggests that dietary factors, especially ultra-processed foods, may pl...

item.title: Tasty&Healthy exclusive whole food diet in asymptomatic children and young adults with biologically active Crohn's disease: the TASTI-E randomized controlled trial.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Tasty&Healthy is an exclusive whole food diet designed to reduce inflammation in Crohn's disease (CD) without the need for formula. This TASTI-E randomized-controlled trial compared the effect of T...

item.title: Update on Diet and Nutritional Therapies in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Review - While the exact cause of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains unclear, interactions among several complex mechanisms are thought to contribute to its pathogenesis. One such factor is diet, whic...

item.title: C3 glomerulopathy associated with anti-factor H autoantibodies in a child with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Pediatric nephrology (Berlin, Germany) - Complement dysregulation may occur secondary to systemic immune activation, but its relationship with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains poorly defined. We report a 10-year-old boy presenting...

item.title: Crosstalk between circRNAs and the classic signaling pathways in IBD.
item.description: RNA biology - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), which includes Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a chronic inflammatory disorder of the gastrointestinal tract with a complex aetiology involvin...

item.title: Fungal β-1,3-glucans: Cell Wall Constituents That Promote Gut Health Through Innate Immune Modulation.
item.description: Nutrients - Review - Fungal β-1,3-glucans are structurally conserved polysaccharide components of the fungal cell wall that exhibit potent immunomodulatory activity. These molecules are recognized by pattern recognitio...

item.title: Dietary Therapies for Gastrointestinal Disorders.
item.description: Nutrients - Review - Alterations in gastrointestinal function (digestion, absorption, motility, secretion, and elimination) play important roles in the pathophysiology of many gastrointestinal disorders. Food also stro...

item.title: Preoperative exclusive enteral nutrition ameliorates CRP-to-albumin ratio without affecting body mass index in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Frontiers in nutrition - Crohn's disease (CD) frequently exhibits malnutrition and systemic inflammation, both of which are closely linked to elevated surgical risks and postoperative complications. Exclusive enteral nutri...

item.title: From nutritional intervention to immune modulation: a multi-database bibliometric and topic modeling study of vitamin D in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Systematic review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic condition characterized by recurrent inflammatory episodes in the gastrointestinal tract. Conventional therapies, including biologics, corticosteroids,...
